frameworks.title: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 — Annex A information security controls
frameworks.note: Control set adopted from ISO/IEC 27002:2022. Titles are the official Annex A control names.

frameworks.title: NIST Cybersecurity Framework 2.0 — Subcategories
frameworks.note: 106 subcategories across 6 Functions and 22 Categories. 'title' holds the official outcome statement.

note: Authoritative reference catalog for external_framework_controls. Seed order: load THIS file first, then control_crosswalks.json (whose external edges reference these controlRefs). The optional 'category' field groups controls for the coverage matrix; persist it if you add a category column, otherwise it may be ignored.

The organizational mission is understood and informs cybersecurity risk management
Internal and external stakeholders, and their needs and expectations regarding cybersecurity risk management, are understood
Legal, regulatory, and contractual requirements regarding cybersecurity, including privacy and civil liberties obligations, are understood and managed
Critical objectives, capabilities, and services that external stakeholders depend on or expect are understood and communicated
Outcomes, capabilities, and services that the organization depends on are understood and communicated
Risk management objectives are established and agreed to by organizational stakeholders
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Risk appetite and risk tolerance statements are established, communicated, and maintained
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Cybersecurity risk management activities and outcomes are included in enterprise risk management processes
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Strategic direction that describes appropriate risk response options is established and communicated
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Lines of communication across the organization are established for cybersecurity risks, including from suppliers and third parties
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: A standardized method for calculating, documenting, categorizing, and prioritizing cybersecurity risks is established and communicated
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Strategic opportunities (i.e., positive risks) are characterized and included in cybersecurity risk discussions
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Risk Management Strategy (GV.RM).

controls.title: Organizational leadership is responsible and accountable for cybersecurity risk and fosters a risk-aware, ethical, continually improving culture
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities (GV.RR).

controls.title: Roles, responsibilities, and authorities related to cybersecurity risk management are established, communicated, understood, and enforced
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities (GV.RR).

controls.title: Adequate resources are allocated commensurate with the cybersecurity risk strategy, roles, responsibilities, and policies
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities (GV.RR).

controls.title: Cybersecurity is included in human resources practices
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Roles, Responsibilities, and Authorities (GV.RR).

controls.title: Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is established based on organizational context, strategy, and priorities and is communicated and enforced
Policy for managing cybersecurity risks is reviewed, updated, communicated, and enforced to reflect changes in requirements, threats, technology, and mission
Cybersecurity risk management strategy outcomes are reviewed to inform and adjust strategy and direction
The cybersecurity risk management strategy is reviewed and adjusted to ensure coverage of organizational requirements and risks
Organizational cybersecurity risk management performance is evaluated and reviewed for adjustments needed
A cybersecurity supply chain risk management program, strategy, objectives, policies, and processes are established and agreed to by stakeholders
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Cybersecurity roles and responsibilities for suppliers, customers, and partners are established, communicated, and coordinated
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management is integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management, risk assessment, and improvement processes
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Suppliers are known and prioritized by criticality
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Requirements to address cybersecurity risks in supply chains are established, prioritized, and integrated into contracts and agreements with suppliers and third parties
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Planning and due diligence are performed to reduce risks before entering into formal supplier or other third-party relationships
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: The risks posed by a supplier, their products and services, and other third parties are understood, recorded, prioritized, assessed, responded to, and monitored over the relationship
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Relevant suppliers and other third parties are included in incident planning, response, and recovery activities
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Supply chain security practices are integrated into cybersecurity and enterprise risk management programs, and performance is monitored across the technology life cycle
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Cybersecurity supply chain risk management plans include provisions for activities that occur after the conclusion of a partnership or service agreement
controls.category: GV.SC Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management
controls.description: Function: Govern (GV); Category: Cybersecurity Supply Chain Risk Management (GV.SC).

controls.title: Inventories of hardware managed by the organization are maintained
Inventories of software, services, and systems managed by the organization are maintained
Representations of the organization's authorized network communication and internal and external network data flows are maintained
Inventories of services provided by suppliers are maintained
Assets are prioritized based on classification, criticality, resources, and impact on the mission
Inventories of data and corresponding metadata for designated data types are maintained
Systems, hardware, software, services, and data are managed throughout their life cycles
Vulnerabilities in assets are identified, validated, and recorded
Cyber threat intelligence is received from information sharing forums and sources
Internal and external threats to the organization are identified and recorded
Potential impacts and likelihoods of threats exploiting vulnerabilities are identified and recorded
Threats, vulnerabilities, likelihoods, and impacts are used to understand inherent risk and inform risk response prioritization
Risk responses are chosen, prioritized, planned, tracked, and communicated
Changes and exceptions are managed, assessed for risk impact, recorded, and tracked
Processes for receiving, analyzing, and responding to vulnerability disclosures are established
The authenticity and integrity of hardware and software are assessed prior to acquisition and use
Critical suppliers are assessed prior to acquisition
Improvements are identified from evaluations
Improvements are identified from security tests and exercises, including those done in coordination with suppliers and third parties
Improvements are identified from execution of operational processes, procedures, and activities
Incident response plans and other cybersecurity plans that affect operations are established, communicated, maintained, and improved
Identities and credentials for authorized users, services, and hardware are managed
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Identities are proofed and bound to credentials based on the context of interactions
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Users, services, and hardware are authenticated
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Identity assertions are protected, conveyed, and verified
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Access permissions, entitlements, and authorizations are defined, managed, enforced, and reviewed, incorporating least privilege and separation of duties
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Physical access to assets is managed, monitored, and enforced commensurate with risk
controls.category: PR.AA Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Identity Management, Authentication, and Access Control (PR.AA).

controls.title: Personnel are provided with awareness and training to perform general tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Awareness and Training (PR.AT).

controls.title: Individuals in specialized roles are provided with awareness and training to perform relevant tasks with cybersecurity risks in mind
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Awareness and Training (PR.AT).

controls.title: The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-at-rest are protected
The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-transit are protected
The confidentiality, integrity, and availability of data-in-use are protected
Backups of data are created, protected, maintained, and tested
Configuration management practices are established and applied
Software is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
Hardware is maintained, replaced, and removed commensurate with risk
Log records are generated and made available for continuous monitoring
Installation and execution of unauthorized software are prevented
Secure software development practices are integrated, and their performance is monitored across the software development life cycle
Networks and environments are protected from unauthorized logical access and usage
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Technology Infrastructure Resilience (PR.IR).

controls.title: The organization's technology assets are protected from environmental threats
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Technology Infrastructure Resilience (PR.IR).

controls.title: Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements in normal and adverse situations
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Technology Infrastructure Resilience (PR.IR).

controls.title: Adequate resource capacity to ensure availability is maintained
controls.description: Function: Protect (PR); Category: Technology Infrastructure Resilience (PR.IR).

controls.title: Networks and network services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
The physical environment is monitored to find potentially adverse events
Personnel activity and technology usage are monitored to find potentially adverse events
External service provider activities and services are monitored to find potentially adverse events
Computing hardware and software, runtime environments, and their data are monitored to find potentially adverse events
Potentially adverse events are analyzed to better understand associated activities
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: Information is correlated from multiple sources
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: The estimated impact and scope of adverse events are understood
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: Information on adverse events is provided to authorized staff and tools
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: Cyber threat intelligence and other contextual information are integrated into the analysis
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: Incidents are declared when adverse events meet the defined incident criteria
controls.description: Function: Detect (DE); Category: Adverse Event Analysis (DE.AE).

controls.title: The incident response plan is executed in coordination with relevant third parties once an incident is declared
Incident reports are triaged and validated
Incidents are categorized and prioritized
Incidents are escalated or elevated as needed
The criteria for initiating incident recovery are applied
Analysis is performed to establish what has taken place during an incident and the root cause
Actions performed during an investigation are recorded, and the records' integrity and provenance are preserved
Incident data and metadata are collected, and their integrity and provenance are preserved
An incident's magnitude is estimated and validated
Internal and external stakeholders are notified of incidents
controls.category: RS.CO Incident Response Reporting and Communication
controls.description: Function: Respond (RS); Category: Incident Response Reporting and Communication (RS.CO).

controls.title: Information is shared with designated internal and external stakeholders
controls.category: RS.CO Incident Response Reporting and Communication
controls.description: Function: Respond (RS); Category: Incident Response Reporting and Communication (RS.CO).

controls.title: Incidents are contained
Incidents are eradicated
The recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed once initiated from the incident response process
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: Recovery actions are selected, scoped, prioritized, and performed
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: The integrity of backups and other restoration assets is verified before using them for restoration
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: Critical mission functions and cybersecurity risk management are considered to establish post-incident operational norms
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: The integrity of restored assets is verified, systems and services are restored, and normal operating status is confirmed
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: The end of incident recovery is declared based on criteria, and incident-related documentation is completed
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Plan Execution (RC.RP).

controls.title: Recovery activities and progress in restoring operational capabilities are communicated to designated stakeholders
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Communication (RC.CO).

controls.title: Public updates on incident recovery are shared using approved methods and messaging
controls.description: Function: Recover (RC); Category: Incident Recovery Communication (RC.CO).
